captured_by: codex-acp gate-closing capture harness (raw newline-delimited JSON-RPC round-trip; AcpBackend SDK types replay the inbound frames). Live AcpBackend e2e (e2e_acp_codex round-trip) also passed against this agent.
redaction: none required — no secrets in the wire frames (codex-acp used the existing ChatGPT login internally; only static auth-method env-var-name description text is present).
note: This file contains ACTUAL JSON-RPC lines emitted by the zed-industries/codex-acp 0.15.0 binary over `codex-acp`. `dir` is the wire direction relative to the bridge (client): send = bridge->agent, recv = agent->bridge. The recv frames are the ones the corpus replay tests feed through AcpBackend's real SDK parse + map path. The agent streamed PONG across two agent_message_chunk frames ("P"+"ONG"); several unmodeled session/update variants (available_commands_update, config_option_update, usage_update) are retained verbatim as captured to prove tolerant-reader DROP behavior.

authMethods.name: Login with ChatGPT
authMethods.description: Use your ChatGPT login with Codex CLI (requires a paid ChatGPT subscription)

availableModes.name: Read Only
availableModes.description: Codex can read files in the current workspace. Approval is required to edit files or access the internet.

availableModes.name: Default
availableModes.description: Codex can read and edit files in the current workspace, and run commands. Approval is required to access the internet or edit other files. (Identical to Agent mode)

availableModes.name: Full Access
availableModes.description: Codex can edit files outside this workspace and access the internet without asking for approval. Exercise caution when using.
